tests.scenario: User logs in and tracks daily mood
steps: The user logs in to the system
The user selects their current mood from the provided options
The user submits the mood entry
tests.expected_result: The system records the user's mood entry for that day

tests.scenario: System records multiple daily mood entries
steps: The user logs in to the system on two consecutive days
On each day, the user selects their current mood and submits the entry
tests.expected_result: The system records a mood entry for each day separately

tests.scenario: User can log emotions easily
steps: Launch the app
Navigate to the emotion tracking section
Select and log a specific emotion (e.g., 'happy')
tests.expected_result: The selected emotion is successfully logged and displayed in the user's emotion history.

tests.scenario: User views logged emotions
steps: Launch the app
Navigate to the emotion tracking section
View logged emotions history
tests.expected_result: The user's logged emotions history is displayed, showing a list of previously logged emotions.

tests.scenario: User views mood history with multiple entries
steps: Log in to the system
Create 5 mood entries with different emotional states over a period of time
Navigate to the mood history page
tests.expected_result: The system displays a visual representation of trends in the user's emotional state over time

tests.scenario: User views mood history with a single entry
steps: Log in to the system
Create 1 mood entry
Navigate to the mood history page
tests.expected_result: The system displays the single mood entry with no trend information

tests.scenario: User views mental health patterns after logging emotional data
steps: Log in as an existing user
Navigate to the emotional data logging section
Log emotional data for at least 3 days
tests.expected_result: The user can view patterns in their mental health when accessing their insights page

tests.scenario: User views mental health patterns with no logged emotional data
steps: Log in as an existing user
Ensure no emotional data has been logged
Navigate to the insights page
tests.expected_result: The user cannot view patterns in their mental health and is prompted to log emotional data

tests.scenario: Successful onboarding and easy mood tracking
steps: Launch the app and complete the onboarding process as a new user
Verify the user is redirected to the main tracking screen
Attempt to track mood by selecting an option
tests.expected_result: The system allows the user to easily select and log their mood

tests.scenario: Onboarding failure still allows tracking
steps: Launch the app and intentionally disrupt the onboarding process
Complete the disrupted onboarding
Attempt to track mood by selecting an option
tests.expected_result: The system still allows the user to track their mood after a disrupted onboarding

tests.scenario: Accessing tools during a depressive episode
steps: Log in to the app as a registered user
Simulate a depressive episode by selecting the 'I'm feeling down' option
Navigate to the 'Manage Episode' section
tests.expected_result: The user can access a list of tools to help manage their depressive episode

tests.scenario: Managing episode without logged-in user
steps: Ensure the user is logged out of the app
Attempt to access the 'Manage Episode' section
Verify the app prompts the user to log in
tests.expected_result: The user is redirected to the login page and cannot access the episode management tools without logging in

tests.scenario: User logs a private entry and verifies it's not shared
steps: Log in as an existing user
Navigate to the private space for self-expression
Create a new private entry with some content
tests.expected_result: The entry is saved and not visible to anyone else

tests.scenario: Saved private entry remains private
steps: Log in as an existing user
Navigate to the private space for self-expression
Create and save a new private entry
Log out and log back in as a different user
tests.expected_result: The saved private entry is not visible to the new user

tests.scenario: User selects English as language
steps: Launch the application
Navigate to the language selection menu
Select English as the preferred language
Save the language selection
tests.expected_result: The application's menu and content are displayed in English.

tests.scenario: User selects Spanish as language
steps: Launch the application
Navigate to the language selection menu
Select Spanish as the preferred language
Save the language selection
tests.expected_result: The application's menu and content are displayed in Spanish.

tests.scenario: User views results without subscription
steps: Log in to the system with valid credentials
Navigate to the results page
Verify that the user has previously logged data
tests.expected_result: The user can view their results without any subscription prompts or restrictions

tests.scenario: User attempts to access results with subscription prompt
steps: Log in to the system with valid credentials
Navigate to a feature that requires a subscription in other parts of the system
Return to the results page without taking the subscription
tests.expected_result: The user can still access and view their results without taking the subscription

tests.scenario: User receives thought-provoking questions during mental health reflection
steps: Launch the mental health app
Navigate to the reflection section
Start the reflection process
tests.expected_result: The user is presented with a thought-provoking question related to their mental health.

tests.scenario: User does not receive questions when not in reflection mode
steps: Launch the mental health app
Navigate to a section other than reflection
Verify the app's content
tests.expected_result: The user is not presented with thought-provoking questions related to their mental health.

tests.scenario: User logs multiple mood entries and views history to see trends
steps: Log in as a registered user
Navigate to the mood tracking section
Log a positive mood entry
Log a negative mood entry on a different day
View mood history
Observe the mood trend over time
tests.expected_result: The system displays a visual representation of the user's mood trend over time, showing both positive and negative entries.

tests.scenario: User views mood history with multiple entries on the same day
steps: Log in as a registered user
Navigate to the mood tracking section
Log a positive mood entry for Monday
Log another positive mood entry for Monday
Log a negative mood entry for Tuesday
View mood history
Observe the mood trend over time
tests.expected_result: The system displays a visual representation of the user's mood trend over time, accurately reflecting multiple entries for the same day and different days.

tests.scenario: User views insights to inform therapy discussions
steps: Log in to the system with a valid user account
Log data for at least one therapy session
Navigate to the insights page
View the insights provided
tests.expected_result: The insights provided can be used to inform therapy discussions.

tests.scenario: System provides valuable insights with logged data
steps: Log in to the system with a valid user account
Log data for multiple therapy sessions
Navigate to the insights page
Review the insights for relevance to therapy discussions
tests.expected_result: The system provides valuable insights based on the logged data.

tests.scenario: User logs personal data and verifies privacy
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the data logging section
Log in with a valid user account
Enter personal data (e.g., health metrics, location)
Save the logged data
tests.expected_result: The logged data is saved and not visible to other users

tests.scenario: User checks data privacy settings
steps: Open the app and go to settings
Navigate to the privacy or data settings section
Verify that data privacy options are available and enabled by default
tests.expected_result: The app confirms that user data is set to private by default

tests.scenario: User can identify patterns after logging emotional data
steps: Log in as an existing user
Navigate to the emotional data logging section
Log emotional data for at least 5 days with varying feelings and behaviors
tests.expected_result: The user can view insights and see identifiable patterns in their behavior and feelings.

tests.scenario: User views insights without logging emotional data
steps: Log in as an existing user
Navigate to the insights section without logging any emotional data
tests.expected_result: The user cannot view insights or see patterns in their behavior and feelings.

tests.scenario: User can log emotions and view history
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the emotion tracking section
Log a new emotion with a description and timestamp
Navigate to the history section and verify the logged emotion is displayed
tests.expected_result: The logged emotion is displayed in the history section with its description and timestamp.

tests.scenario: User can view logged emotions
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the emotion tracking section
Log three new emotions with different descriptions and timestamps
Navigate to the history section and verify all logged emotions are displayed in chronological order
tests.expected_result: All logged emotions are displayed in the history section in chronological order with their descriptions and timestamps.
